learning_outcomes.description: analyse the results of the independent clinical project and discuss them in relation to Magnetic Resonance Technology
to collect, analyse and organise information and convey that information clearly and fluently
use the theory you have learned during your Magnetic Resonance Technology program to conduct an independent clinical MRI project

assessments.task_description: Project proposal (2-3 pages), must include aim, justification, method, equipment and bibliography.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
PowerPoint presentation of the project. This should be presented to a group of your peers on Friday 30th October.

If you receive an approved extension for the PowerPoint Slides please contact the Course Coordinator to arrange a date/time for the presentation, generally held the day after the slides are submitted
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students are permitted to submit a draft prior to final submission. The PowerPoint presentation should be submitted via Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Dissertation (absolute maximum of 15,000 words).  Should follow standard format:

-Introduction & literature review that finishes with aim & hypothesis
-Method
-Results
-Discussion & Imaging Portfolio
-Conclusion
-Bibliography
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students are permitted to submit a draft prior to final submission. Please submit your dissertation to TurnItIn, through BlackBoard (https://learn.uq.edu.au ). Detailed instructions on how to access and use TurnItIn will be provided at the beginning of the semester. The assignment should be saved as a word or a pdf file. The file name should be MRES7015_surname_dis.doc. The first page of your assignment must be the assignment coversheet filled with your details. The coversheet is available for downloading on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
